title: veronica incana subsp. pallens
intro: Veronica incana subsp. pallens is a subspecies of the Veronica incana plant, commonly known as the hoary speedwell, which is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Veronica incana subsp. pallens include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, including eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Veronica incana subsp. pallens can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and crushing the leaves to make a powder that can be used in various medicinal applications.
